title: PHP Source
documentation:
## Purpose
PHP source files contain code written in PHP, a widely deployed server-side scripting language designed primarily for web development. PHP is the language underlying WordPress, Drupal, Laravel, and a large portion of the web's server-side infrastructure.

## When to use
Use [PHP Source](https://capdag.com/browse/media:php;textable;code) for `.php` files and `.phtml` files (PHP-enhanced HTML templates). PHP files typically contain a mix of PHP code delimited by `<?php ... ?>` tags and HTML, though modern frameworks use pure PHP class files without HTML embedding.

## Notes
PHP 7 and PHP 8 introduced substantial changes including typed properties, named arguments, enums, and fibers; the PHP version must be known to correctly interpret syntax. PHP source is UTF-8 text. The `declare(strict_types=1)` directive at the top of a file enables strict type checking and affects how the file should be analyzed for type correctness.
